channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump, Zelenskyy to meet for key deal as NATO allies, Russia wait, watch
item.description: The world waits to see what could come from a key deal between Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and President Trump that could open Kyiv's minerals to the U.S. in exchange for security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope reaches 2-week mark at hospital with pneumonia and doctors suggest he's overcome the worst
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis reached the two-week mark recovery from pneumonia Friday as doctors suggested he had overcome the most critical phase of the infection while holding out on declaring him out of danger entirely.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel's army admits failures on Oct. 7. Its probe of the attack could put pressure on Netanyahu
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — An investigation by the Israeli military has determined that Hamas was able to carry out the deadliest attack in Israeli history on Oct. 7, 2023, because the much more powerful Israeli army misjudged the militant group’s intentions and underestimated its capabilities.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN talks end in Rome with nations backing $200 billion a year plan to protect nature
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Global negotiators concluded an extended session of the United Nations biodiversity conference, COP16, with key commitments on funds needed and the institutions through which the funds will be channeled to protect the world's biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama and Costa Rica turning into a 'black hole' for migrants and deportees from US, observers warn
item.description: Officials in Costa Rica and Panama are confiscating migrants’ passports and cellphones, denying them access to legal services and moving them between remote outposts as they wrestle with the logistics of a suddenly reversed migration flow.
item.dc:creator: Megan Janetsky, Matías Delacroix And Joshua Goodman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tens of thousands of Palestinians seek shelter after Israeli assaults across the West Bank
item.description: Nearly 40,000 Palestinians driven from their homes by Israel's month-long offensive in the West Bank have scattered across nearby villages and towns, trying to find shelter.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Aref Tufaha And Jalal Bwaitel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Doug Ford reelected as Ontario’s premier with mandate to fight Trump tariffs
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Ontario Premier Doug Ford won reelection to lead Canada’s most populous province on Thursday, giving him the mandate he wanted to fight tariffs threatened by U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea says it performed cruise missile tests, days after it vowed to respond to US threats
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Friday it had test-fired strategic cruise missiles to demonstrate its nuclear counter-attack capability, days after it vowed to respond to what it called escalating U.S.-led hostilities since the start of the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the latest effort to end Turkey's 40-year Kurdish conflict
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — The imprisoned leader of the Kurdistan Workers' Party has called on his organization to disarm and dissolve as part of a new effort to end 40 years of fighting between the state and the banned militant group.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US wants to end Ukraine war with little involvement: Ex-ambassador
item.description: President Donald Trump has met with European leaders during the week to discuss ending the war quickly.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Baier to interview Zelensky after Trump meeting
item.description: Bret Baier has secured the first one-on-one interview with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky after his meeting with President Trump at the White House, to discuss the ongoing Ukraine-Russia conflict.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Republicans block measure condemning Trump administration vote on UN Russia resolution
item.description: Senate Republicans on Thursday objected to an effort by Democrats to condemn the Trump administration's decision to vote against a United Nations resolution laying blame for the war in Ukraine at the feet of Russia. Sen. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, The Hill

item.title: Trump lays out Ukraine minerals deal ahead of Zelensky meeting
item.description: President Trump on Thursday offered more details about the mineral agreement the U.S. is set to sign with Ukraine, arguing it would allow America to recoup aid provided to Kyiv, while boosting Ukraine’s economy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: The Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, arrive in Florida
item.description: After the brothers arrived in Florida, state Attorney General James Uthmeier said in a social media post that his office would conduct a “preliminary inquiry” into them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis' condition continues to improve as he receives oxygen therapy: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is improving as he undergoes high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said in a statement. The 88-year-old pontiff has been hospitalized for nearly two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles invites Trump to UK for a state visit
item.description: King Charles III has invited President Trump for a state visit to the United Kingdom, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the president during their meeting Thursday in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis continued to improve from double pneumonia Thursday, the Vatican said, working from his hospital room and going to his private chapel to pray, though doctors said he needs more days of “clinical stability” before they revise their guarded prognosis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schiff: Millions of Americans 'downright ashamed' of Trump
item.description: Sen. Adam Schiff (D-Calif.), a longtime critic of President Trump, said he thinks millions of Americans are ashamed of the president “for the first time” over his efforts to strike a deal with Ukraine to gain access to its mineral wealth.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump: Wayne Gretzky supports independent Canada, should not be criticized
item.description: Wayne Gretzky, a longtime friend and supporter of President Trump, does not support Trump's vision of Canada becoming the 51st U.S. state, but the two remain on good terms.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: North Korea appears to have sent more troops to Russia to back its war against Ukraine, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s spy agency said Thursday that North Korea appears to have sent additional troops to Russia, after its soldiers deployed on the Russian-Ukraine fronts suffered heavy casualties.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa and their dog were dead for some time, warrant shows
item.description: A search warrant shows that Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and one of their dogs had been dead for some time when a maintenance worker discovered their bodies at the couple's Santa Fe, New Mexico, home.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 hostages to Israel as dozens of Palestinians leave Israeli prison
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas handed over the bodies of four hostages to the Red Cross early Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, days before the first phase of the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip was to end.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Romanian far-right presidential frontrunner taken into custody after JD Vance's rebuke of European elections
item.description: Romanian candidate Calin Georgescu is being questioned by prosecutors after Vice President JD Vance criticized a "Soviet-era" shift in European democracies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan dispatches navy, air force after China launches live-fire drills with no warning
item.description: Taiwan's defense ministry says China launched live-fire drills barely 40 miles off the coast of Taiwan on Wednesday with no prior warning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas agrees to return bodies of 4 Israeli hostages in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners
item.description: Hamas has agreed to return the bodies of more dead Israeli hostages with just days to go before the end of the first phase of the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Analysts back Trump’s USAID cuts in Africa, say increased trade will better benefit continent's poor
item.description: Some analysts believe that President Donald Trump was right to cut aid to Africa and that increased trade with the continent will fill "the plates of hungry kids at the end of the day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Funeral held for Shiri Bibas and her sons after their remains handed over by Hamas
item.description: A funeral procession for a mother and her two young sons whose remains were handed over by Hamas is being held Wednesday morning in Rishon Lezion, Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New search to begin for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370: Reports
item.description: The latest search comes after the Malaysian government in December agreed to accept a second "no find, no fee" proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US refuses to blame Russia for Ukraine war, splitting with European allies in UN votes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions Monday seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis shows 'slight improvement' in kidney failure battle
item.description: Pope Francis' 10th night at Gemelli Hospital in Rome was quiet and restful as he battles early signs of kidney failure, the Vatican said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
